description: This figure presents comprehensive transmission electron microscopy (TEM) characterization of the Fe-35Mn-15Al-5Cu (15Al) low-density steel aged at 600°C for 6 h. Panel (a) shows low-magnification bright-field (BF) and corresponding dark-field (DF) images revealing complete disappearance of the α matrix and formation of abundant near-equiaxed β-Mn grains. Panel (b) displays higher magnification BF imaging of β-Mn particles alongside selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns from circled regions: the green circle confirms the β-Mn matrix structure with [012] beam axis, while orange and cyan circles from fine particles at interfaces show FCC crystalline structure with [011] beam axis and visible FCC twins. Panel (c) presents a BF image with corresponding EDS elemental mapping (Fe, Mn, Al, Cu), demonstrating that the interfacial FCC phase is rich in Cu (magenta) while depleted in Fe and Mn, whereas β-Mn is poor in Cu. These results elucidate the microstructural origin of ultra-high specific hardness through complete β-Mn transformation and Cu-rich FCC precipitate formation.
